bids.reasoning: This is the opening at t=0.0 with no opponent offers or messages to infer their preferences, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. With no time pressure yet, I should anchor strongly on my best values across all issues.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, and the opponent’s only offer is the minimum-utility bid for me on every issue, so there is no evidence of meaningful concession yet. I am targeting a very high utility while avoiding an exact repeat of my opening bid, conceding only on my lowest-weight issueA and holding firm on the more important issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.04 and the opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueA offer. I will make only a small concession on my lowest-weight issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.95 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has mostly held firm at very low-value offers for me, with only a small movement on issueC, so I will concede only on my least important issueA while holding my high-weight issues B, C, and D at valueF.

I am targeting very high utility because it is still early (t=0.08) and the opponent has mostly held at very low-value bids for me, with only small movement on issueA/issueC. Since issueA is my lowest-weight issue and the opponent just offered valueB there, I can concede on issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and especially D.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a meaningful concession on my lowest-weight issue. The opponent has only made small concessions and their offers remain low utility for me, but they have repeatedly pushed issueA low, so I concede issueA while holding firm on the more important issues B, C, and D.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.888, since it is still early at t=0.12 and the opponent has only made small concessions from very low-utility offers. Their latest bid improves slightly on issueA, issueB, and issueD, but remains far from acceptable for me, so I will concede on the lower-weight issueB while holding firm on the more important issueC and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.856 because it is still early at t=0.14, so I should concede slowly while protecting the high-weight issues C and D. The opponent’s latest offer improved sharply for me by giving issueD: valueF, but they are still offering weak values on A and B, so I will make a small concession on issueB while holding issueC and issueD at my preferred valueF.

I am targeting a strong early-game utility around 0.82, since time is still only 0.16 and there is no need to concede too deeply yet. The opponent has started conceding on issueD and issueC in their latest bids, but I should still hold firm on my highest-weight issues D and C while conceding more on the lower-weight issues A and B.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.78 since it is early at t=0.18, so I should not concede heavily yet. The opponent’s offers have improved for me mainly on issueD and issueB while staying relatively tough on issueA/issueC, so I keep my highest-weight issueD at valueF, keep issueB at their latest concession level, and make a modest concession on issueC rather than repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues while preserving strong outcomes on issueC and especially issueD. At t=0.20 there is still enough time to avoid matching the opponent’s low offers, though their bids have improved somewhat for me, especially on issueD. Their latest offer is still below what I should accept, so I will make a modest concession from my previous bid by lowering issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, still well above reservation because it is early-mid negotiation at t=0.22 and there is no need to make deep concessions yet. The opponent has begun moving on issueB and issueC but is inconsistent on issueD, so I will protect high-weight issueC/issueD while offering a different tradeoff from my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.71, since it is only t=0.24 and there is no need to drop close to the opponent’s low recent offers yet. The opponent has shown some willingness on issueB and occasionally issueD/issueC, but their last offer fell back on high-weight issueD, so I will concede mainly on lower and medium-weight issues while holding issueD at valueE.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.744 because it is early-mid negotiation at t=0.26, so I should not concede too deeply yet. The opponent has recently shown willingness to offer issueD=valueE and has previously offered issueB=valueD and issueC=valueE, so I combine those signals while conceding my lowest-weight issueA to valueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier bids but still well above reservation at t=0.28. The opponent’s latest offer improved to high value on issueC and medium-high on issueD, so I am accepting their signal on issueC while asking for a stronger issueB and keeping the overall package favorable to me.

I am targeting a utility around 0.76, which is a moderate concession from my initial maximum while still protecting the high-weight issues C and D. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s latest low-utility bid; their offers show movement on issueD and occasional movement on issueC, so I am matching those signals while conceding more on lower-weight issues A and B.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.71: still protecting the high-weight issues issueD and issueC at valueE, while conceding more on the lower-weight issueA and issueB. At t=0.32 there is some time left, but the opponent’s offers have improved from all valueA to bids with issueC/issueD around valueD, so I will make a constructive concession without dropping to their last offer.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70, which is a moderate concession from my prior offers but still protects my highest-weight issues, especially issueC and issueD. At t=0.34 there is no need to drop to the opponent’s weakest bids, but their history shows occasional willingness to give high issueC and mid issueD, so I am proposing a tradeoff close to signals they have already made. Their latest offer regressed, so I am not making a large concession, just a new non-identical bid with some movement on issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.68, conceding further on the lower-weight issueB while preserving good outcomes on the higher-weight issueC and issueD. At t=0.36 there is still time, but the opponent’s offers have improved unevenly and they recently signaled willingness on issueC=valueE and earlier on issueD=valueE, so this combines concessions with a still-profitable package.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73, since we are still before the midpoint and should not drop to the opponent’s recent ~0.57-utility offers yet. The opponent has shown some willingness to give high values on issueD and occasionally issueC, while mostly pressing low values on issueA/issueB, so I concede on the lower-weight issues and hold more firmly on issueC/issueD.

I am targeting a utility around 0.71, which is a moderate concession from my earlier bids while still preserving value on the most important issueD. At t=0.40 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low latest offer; their offers show occasional willingness on issueC/issueD, so I concede on low-weight issueA and some on issueB/issueC while holding issueD at valueE.

I am targeting about 0.70 utility, conceding further on the lower-weight issueA and partially on issueB/issueC while protecting issueD, my most important issue. At t=0.42 we are in mid-game, so I should move from my prior stance without dropping to the opponent’s much lower offers; their history shows some willingness to offer issueD:valueE and issueB:valueD, but they remain inconsistent on issueC.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.68, conceding modestly from my prior offers while still protecting the highest-weight issueD and issueC. The opponent’s offers are inconsistent but have sometimes included favorable values on issueB and issueC, while issueD remains the main sticking point, so I concede issueD only to valueD rather than their low valueA. This bid avoids repeating my last exact offer and stays well above reservation value at t=0.44.

I am targeting a mid-game utility of about 0.63, conceding meaningfully but still protecting the most important issueD. The opponent has not shown steady concession, but they have repeatedly offered stronger values on issueB and sometimes issueC/issueD, so I am matching their latest issueB=valueD while moving issueC and issueD to valueD as a plausible compromise.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65, conceding mainly on low-weight issueA and some on issueB while protecting higher-weight issueC and issueD. The opponent has not steadily improved, but they have previously offered valueE on issueC and valueD/valueE on issueD, so this bid leans toward values they have signaled may be feasible without dropping too far for me.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.62, conceding substantially from my ideal while still protecting my highest-weight issueD and issueC at valueD. The opponent’s offers have been inconsistent but their latest bid includes issueC: valueD and issueA: valueB, so I am using those signals while asking for movement on issueB and issueD. With time at 0.50, I should not drop to the opponent’s low-utility latest bid yet, but I should avoid repeating my prior offer and keep concessions credible.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.62, which concedes meaningfully from my earlier positions while staying above the best recent opponent offers to me. The opponent’s bids are inconsistent but their latest offer included issueB:valueC and issueD:valueE, so I keep those and ask for a moderate improvement on issueC while conceding issueA fully.

I am targeting a mid-game compromise around 0.57 utility for me, conceding mainly on lower-weight issueA and issueB while preserving reasonable value on the more important issueC and issueD. The opponent has not consistently conceded but has previously offered issueC:valueD and issueD:valueD together, so this bid uses a configuration they have already signaled may be tolerable; there are no messages to weigh.
